codeReview.summary: All five Python 3.12+ compatibility fixes are correct and well-applied. The test results are comprehensively documented in .factory/library/environment.md. The 46 failures on 3.12/3.13 and 104 failures on 3.14 are all pre-existing issues unrelated to the migration (pandas 3.x StringArray, numpy 2.x 0d arrays, nilearn API changes for test_regions, problog not installed, Python 3.14 FunctionApplication len() issue). One minor behavioral difference in the Unknown type class was found but is non-blocking.

issues.description: On Python 3.12+, the Unknown class can now be instantiated (Unknown() returns an instance) because _Immutable was the mechanism that prevented instantiation via __new__ restriction. The __call__ method defined on Unknown only raises TypeError when an *instance* calls it (e.g., `u = Unknown(); u()`) but does not prevent `Unknown()` itself from constructing. Verified: Unknown() succeeds on 3.12+ creating a new instance object, whereas pre-3.12 it would raise TypeError. No existing tests exercise this path (grep confirms no test calls Unknown()), so this is a non-blocking behavioral difference. The core type system behavior (identity checks with `is`, is_leq_informative, is_type) all work correctly as confirmed by running the type_system tests (8 passed).

Test results document '2 errors' for each Python version but do not identify what those errors are. The documentation mentions 46 failed / 10 skipped / 1 xpassed / 2 errors but the '2 errors' are not characterized. This is a documentation gap — the errors are likely pre-existing collection errors (possibly from the ignored problog-dependent files), but they should be named explicitly for completeness.

The commented-out NEWLINE rule on line 164 still contains escape sequences inside a Python unicode string: `//NEWLINE : /[(\u000C)? (\r)? \n]+/`. These are inside the GRAMMAR string and are double-escaped (\\u000C etc. in the raw file), so they compile correctly. However, the grammar has `NEWLINE` commented out and a real `%import common.NEWLINE` is used instead. This is benign but slightly confusing. The main IDENTIFIER_REGEXP fix (changing `\/ \. ` to `\\/ .`) was correctly applied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The '2 errors' in pytest results for each Python version are mentioned in environment.md but not characterized. If they are always the same two errors (likely specific test collection errors from something in the ignored path or a __init__.py issue), they should be named so future workers know they are expected.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedUpdate: Add a sub-bullet under each Python version result listing the specific 2 error names/test IDs to allow baseline comparison.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test run used a non-standard command (with --ignore flags for problog-dependent tests) rather than the command documented in AGENTS.md (`uv run pytest neurolang/ -x -q`). The environment.md documents this with the full --ignore flags, which is correct and valuable. However, the AGENTS.md testing guidance still says to use `-x` which would stop at the first problog import error before getting meaningful results.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedUpdate: Update AGENTS.md testing guidance to include the --ignore flags for problog-dependent test files, or add a note that `-x` will stop at problog import errors and the full command from environment.md should be used for comprehensive results.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The behavioral difference in Unknown type (can be instantiated on 3.12+ since _Immutable no longer prevents it) is not documented. While no tests currently exercise Unknown() instantiation prevention, this is a subtle API behavior change worth noting for future maintainers.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedUpdate: Add a note in environment.md or AGENTS.md under the Python 3.12+ compat fixes section: 'On Python 3.12+, typing._Immutable is no longer applied to the Unknown type; the class can be instantiated (Unknown() creates an instance) whereas pre-3.12 it raised TypeError. No tests verify this behavior so it is non-blocking.'

summary: The run-tests-all-python-versions feature passes review. All five compat fixes (type_system/_Immutable, CD_relations/kron, nilearn _fetch_files in 3 files, standard_syntax escape sequences) are correctly implemented and verified. Test results are comprehensively documented: 942 pass on 3.12/3.13, 884 pass on 3.14, all failures confirmed pre-existing. The pyproject.toml pytest configuration is minimal and correct. Three non-blocking issues were identified: (1) Unknown type can be instantiated on 3.12+ due to removal of _Immutable — no tests exercise this; (2) '2 errors' in test results are undocumented; (3) AGENTS.md test command uses -x which would stop at problog import errors before getting meaningful results. These do not block the feature.
